lesson_title: Friend classes and friend member functions

cards.text: A {{c1::friend class}} is a class whose members can all access the private and protected members of another class.
To make an entire class a friend, declare it with: {{c1::friend class}} ClassName;
Friendship is {{c1::not reciprocal}} - if class A is a friend of class B, class B is {{c2::not automatically}} a friend of class A.
Friendship is {{c1::not transitive}} - if A is a friend of B, and B is a friend of C, A is {{c2::not automatically}} a friend of C.
To make only a specific member function a friend: friend ReturnType {{c1::ClassName::}}memberFunction();

cards.front: What is the difference between a friend class and a friend function?
cards.back:
• Friend class: ALL members of the class can access private data
  • Friend function: Only that specific function can access private data

cards.front: If class Storage makes class Display a friend, can Display access Storage's private members?
cards.back: Yes. Display's member functions can access all private and protected members of Storage.

cards.question: Complete the friend class declaration.
cards.explanation: Declare Display as a friend class inside Storage to grant all Display members access to Storage's private data.

cards.question: If A is a friend of B, and B is a friend of C, can A access C's private members?
cards.answer: No, this is invalid. Friendship is not transitive.
cards.explanation: A can access B's members, B can access C's members, but A cannot access C's members.

cards.question: How do you make only the print() member function of Display a friend?
cards.explanation: Use ClassName::functionName syntax to make only a specific member function a friend.

When using friend classes:
  • Use sparingly - they tightly couple classes together
  • Prefer friend functions over friend classes when possible
  • Remember friendship is not inherited by derived classes
  • Document why the friendship is necessary
  • Consider if the design could be improved to avoid friends
examples: friend class LinkedList;  // List needs Node internals
